{{Quote|The so-called power gems were used by the space pirates of Iridium!|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]]|<ref name="Power Gem" />}}
'''Iridium''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was home to the infamous [[Iridium Pirates/Legends|Pirates of Iridium]], who utilized Iridium "[[power gem]]s" to disable the [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] of their victims' [[Starship/Legends|starships]], raiding Atrivis [[Hyperspace route/Legends|space lanes]] for a [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. However, a task force of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Judicial/Legends|Judicials]] routed the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in a [[Skirmish over Vuchelle|battle]] over [[Vuchelle]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].

==History==
{{Quote|The Judicial Department confirm that a Jedi task force has thrashed the space pirates of Iridium, bringing an end to their year-long raids along the Atrivis sector routes.|[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49]]|<ref name="HNN" />}}
[[File:Iridium_pirate.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Pirates of Iridium attack a starship.]]
Iridium was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|"Iridium"}}</ref> located in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="HNN" /> which was part of the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] region.<ref name="Region" /> In the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] leading up to the pan-galactic [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the planet was home to the [[Iridium Pirates/Legends|Pirates of Iridium]]. The [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] raided the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade routes]] of the Atrivis sector for a year, taking [[Grain/Legends|grain]] and [[Spice/Legends|spice]] shipments.<ref name="HNN" /> The infamous pirates made use of Iridium "[[power gem]]s," which generated an aura capable of disrupting [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]. This gave them a significant advantage in their raids,<ref name="SWE" /> until they were routed by a force of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] and [[Judicial/Legends|Republic Judiciaries]] at a [[Skirmish over Vuchelle|battle]] over the planet [[Vuchelle]].<ref name="HNN" />

Iridium fell within the borders of the [[Pentastar Alignment]] between the years [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].<ref name="Atlas" />

==Inhabitants==
During the early years of the Clone Wars, Iridium was inhabited by a pirate group known to include [[Human/Legends|Humans]] such as [[Raskar]].<ref name="Power Gem" /> They were believed by [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] sources to be working as privateers for the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], and made use of [[Longprow attack cruiser]], [[2 Warpod|#2 Warpod pinnaces]], and [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]; however they were completely routed and dispersed in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] at Vuchelle.<ref name="HNN" />

==Behind the scenes==
Iridium was first mentioned in ''[[The Power Gem]]'', a weekly comic strip written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Al Williamson]], published weekly by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] from July to October 1982. The strip never specified Iridium was a planet, only using it as part of the organization known as "the Pirates of Iridium;" however, the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' later confirmed its status as a planet. The [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] website again referenced the planet in the build-up to the release of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], detailing the destruction of the Pirates first mentioned in the initial comic strip. It was later included in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''.

The name of the planet, "Iridium," appears to be a reference to the [[Wikipedia:Iridium|real-world element]] found in meteorites.
